subtopics.title: Types and Uses of Simple Machines
common_misconceptions: Simple machines are only for simple tasks
They reduce the amount of work done
subtopics.teaching_note: Simple machines make work easier by changing direction or magnitude of force. Lever: seesaw, crowbar, scissors (fulcrum, effort, load). Three classes: 1st (fulcrum in middle), 2nd (load in middle), 3rd (effort in middle). Pulley: flagpole, construction. Inclined plane: ramp, staircase. Wedge: knife, axe. Screw: bolt, jar lid. Wheel and axle: bicycle, car, door knob. Mechanical advantage = output force / input force. Nigerian examples: grinding stone (lever), local palm oil press.
